summary: The United States launched additional defensive strikes against multiple targets in southern Iran, including Asaluyeh and Kangan Naval Base, while Israeli forces reported strikes on Hezbollah infrastructure in southern Lebanon. Iranian air defense systems were activated in Asaluyeh and Tehran, and several explosions were reported in Iranian provinces such as Bushehr, Hormozgan, and Fars. Hezbollah and Iranian forces continued exchanges of fire in Lebanon and the Strait of Hormuz region.
key_points: US forces conducted defensive strikes on multiple Iranian targets, including Asaluyeh and Kangan Naval Base, with three hits reported on the naval base (Source: @shin_persian/6890).
Iranian air defense systems were activated in Asaluyeh and Tehran, with reports of air defense activity and explosions in the vicinity (Source: @presstv/193557; Source: @shin_persian/6883).

Two blasts occurred in Sirik, Hormozgan Province, and additional explosions were reported in Bushehr and Fars provinces (Source: @shin_persian/6877; Source: @asafroz/50136; Source: @SharghDaily/165144).
The IDF struck six Hezbollah sites in Tyre, Lebanon, including a drone launch facility, and reported at least 13 Lebanese casualties (Source: @manniefabian/58532; Source: @manniefabian/58538).
A suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering sirens in the Israeli border community of Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
Iranian forces engaged US aircraft in the Strait of Hormuz area, with reports of missile launches from Tabriz, Kermanshah, and Khorramabad (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368750).
US officials warned of hundreds of targets to be hit near the Strait of Hormuz later that night (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73251).

sections.executive_summary: US defensive strikes hit multiple Iranian sites, including three hits on Kangan Naval Base, while Iranian air defenses were activated in Asaluyeh. Israeli forces intensified operations against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, striking six sites in Tyre and intercepting a drone. Regional tensions rose with missile launches near the Strait of Hormuz and reported explosions across several Iranian provinces.

points: Air defense activity was reported in Asaluyeh, Bushehr Province, with local sources confirming activation of air defense systems (Source: @shin_persian/6883).
Two explosions were recorded in Sirik, Hormozgan Province, indicating possible strikes or internal incidents (Source: @shin_persian/6877).
Explosions and air defense sounds were reported in the city of Mehr, Fars Province, though no confirmed blast occurred within the city limits (Source: @SharghDaily/165144).
Local sources stated that Kangan Naval Base was hit three times during recent US operations (Source: @shin_persian/6890).
Multiple missile launches were claimed from Tabriz, Kermanshah, and Khorramabad, suggesting heightened Iranian missile activity (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368750).
Iranian officials warned that any new aggression would be met with a harsh response, underscoring a readiness to retaliate (Source: @presstv/193561).
The IDF reported striking six Hezbollah sites in Tyre, including a facility used for launching explosive drones (Source: @manniefabian/58532).
At least 13 people were reported killed in Israeli strikes on Hezbollah targets in Lebanon (Source: @manniefabian/58538).
A suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering civil defense sirens in Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
IDF statements indicated ongoing destruction of Hezbollah terror infrastructure, including rocket launchers and drone launch sites in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24270).
Israeli forces continued operations against Hezbollah in the town of Sour, targeting six infrastructure sites used for drone attacks (Source: @idf_telegram/24265).
Israeli strikes hit Hezbollah infrastructure in Tyre and other southern Lebanese areas, including a drone launch facility (Source: @manniefabian/58532).
Hezbollah operatives were reported killed, with at least 13 casualties from recent Israeli attacks (Source: @manniefabian/58538).
A Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, causing alarms in the Israeli border town of Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
IDF forces reported destroying multiple Hezbollah rocket launchers and weapon caches in southern Lebanon (Source: @idf_telegram/24270).
Operations in the town of Sour targeted six Hezbollah infrastructure sites used for drone launches (Source: @idf_telegram/24265).
US Embassy in Baghdad issued a security alert urging heightened readiness for Americans due to regional developments (Source: @Faytuks_Network/3755).
US officials indicated that the Central Command would be busy tonight with strikes on Iranian facilities, suggesting possible operations extending into Iraq or Syria (Source: @naya_foriraq/78197).
Pakistani source reported that US Navy ships are targeting a base in Sirik, indicating naval activity in the Gulf region (Source: @pkpoi/142665).
US CENTCOM announced the launch of strikes against multiple targets in Iran, emphasizing a broader campaign in the Gulf (Source: @presstv/193561).
A US official stated that hundreds of targets near the Strait of Hormuz would be attacked, indicating intensified pressure on Gulf shipping lanes (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73251).
Trump claimed that over 100 million barrels of oil passed through the Strait of Hormuz under a secret US mission, highlighting strategic control of the waterway (Source: @VahidOnline/76158).

key_developments: US forces conducted defensive strikes on multiple Iranian targets, including three hits on Kangan Naval Base (Source: @shin_persian/6890).
Iranian air defense systems were activated in Asaluyeh, Bushehr Province, following reported explosions (Source: @shin_persian/6883).
Two blasts were reported in Sirik, Hormozgan Province, indicating ongoing hostilities (Source: @shin_persian/6877).
The IDF struck six Hezbollah sites in Tyre, Lebanon, including a drone launch facility (Source: @manniefabian/58532).
A suspected Hezbollah drone was intercepted over southern Lebanon, triggering sirens in Shlomi (Source: @manniefabian/58536).
Iranian sources reported missile launches from Tabriz, Kermanshah, and Khorramabad (Source: @rasedal3ado138e/368750).
US officials warned that hundreds of targets near the Strait of Hormuz would be attacked later that night (Source: @rodast_omiddana/73251).
sections.threat_assessment: The United States is escalating its campaign against Iranian infrastructure, employing air and naval strikes that have engaged Iranian air defenses and prompted missile launches. Israel continues a focused offensive against Hezbollah in southern Lebanon, targeting drone launch sites and rocket capabilities, which raises the risk of broader retaliation. Iranian missile activity near the Strait of Hormuz and reported engagements with US aircraft suggest a widening of the conflict into critical maritime corridors.
sections.regional_response: Regional actors, including Gulf states, are monitoring US strike activity closely, with statements emphasizing control over oil transit through the Strait of Hormuz. Iraqi authorities have issued alerts for heightened security, reflecting concerns over spillover effects. Hezbollah's continued operations and Israeli counterstrikes maintain a volatile front in southern Lebanon, influencing Lebanese internal security dynamics.
sections.intelligence_notes: Open-source reports confirm multiple US strikes on Iranian targets, corroborated by local eyewitness accounts of air defense activation. Israeli military communications provide detailed accounts of Hezbollah infrastructure degradation, including drone interception. Iranian missile launch claims require further verification but indicate a possible escalation in asymmetric capabilities targeting regional shipping lanes.
